description: Priya · Senior Backend Engineer · Bi-weekly — May-24 qspec regression (547a1f92-945)

prep.name: Priya
prep.notes: Priya shipped a major feature on Monday and the team noticed, but she has been quieter this week. I want to check whether this is normal post-launch recovery or something unresolved. She also mentioned wanting to mentor a few months ago and that thread never moved. I need a clearer read on what she expects to own next quarter before I introduce the billing rewrite, since she may already be forming plans that this will disrupt.

focusPoints.label: Pace and headspace after Monday's ship.
focusPoints.reason: Notes say she's quieter than usual this week, right after a major feature landed. Could be a normal post-launch exhale, could be something else — worth asking how she's actually feeling before moving on.

focusPoints.label: The Monday launch — making sure it landed for her.
focusPoints.reason: Team noticed the work, but that's not the same as Priya feeling seen. Worth naming it directly and hearing what she's proud of, not just confirming the team clapped.

focusPoints.label: The mentoring interest she floated three months ago.
focusPoints.reason: She mentioned wanting to mentor in passing and it hasn't come up since. That's easy to let drift. A bi-weekly is a good moment to ask whether that's still something she wants to pursue and what would make it real.

focusPoints.label: What she's expecting to own next quarter.
focusPoints.reason: Whether she's already forming ideas about what comes next matters — she's about to be pulled into a rewrite she doesn't know about yet, and understanding her current expectations will shape how that conversation lands when you have it.

answers: The launch went well, but this week I feel flatter than I expected.
Mostly cleanup and reviews right now, not much that feels new.
I am proud of the migration plan and how we handled the cutover.
I did mention mentoring before. I still want it, but I stopped pushing.
I am not sure what I am owning next quarter yet, and that makes me uneasy.
I have felt a bit stuck doing similar work for months.
If there is a bigger scope conversation, I want it sooner rather than later.
A clear next-step path would help me reset my energy.

may24Bank.name: Now that Monday's ship is behind you, where is your head actually at?
Did the recognition from Monday land the way you'd have wanted, or did anything feel missing?
What specifically made this launch go well that you'd want to carry into the next piece of work?
What are you currently waiting on that could quietly stall your progress in the next few weeks?
What are you expecting to own next quarter — and what's shaping that expectation?
What's the one thing that feels like it's genuinely moving forward right now?
You mentioned wanting to mentor a while back — is that still something you want to pursue, or has that shifted?
What would you need to drop or reduce to actually make space for mentoring in your week?
Does the current pace feel like something you could keep up for another couple of months without it costing you?
Given everything we've talked about, what's the one thing you want to have moved by the time we next meet?

brief.coreIssue: Priya needs to discuss what specific challenges she faces after the launch and how those challenges might impact her performance going forward.
brief.openingQuestion: What specific post-launch challenges have you faced that might impact your transition to the next level?
listenFor: if she has a plan to address performance issues
brief.goodOutcome: You and Priya have agreed on one specific skill to improve this quarter.
brief.suggestedAction: Schedule a follow-up meeting next month to review progress on the agreed skill.

brief.coreIssue: Priya shipped a major backend feature and is quieter this week — check post-launch energy before next-quarter ownership and the mentoring thread.
brief.openingQuestion: What's it actually felt like this week, now that Monday's ship is behind you?
listenFor: whether she names depletion, relief, or something unresolved about the launch
if she volunteers the mentoring thread or lights up when you raise it
whether she names concrete expectations for next quarter's ownership
avoid: do not introduce the billing rewrite in this meeting
do not let recognition become a quick checkbox
brief.goodOutcome: You and Priya agree on one concrete mentoring step for a Senior Backend Engineer — a named pairing, guild slot, or timeline — before the next bi-weekly.
brief.suggestedAction: During the 1:1, ask Priya to name one person she would mentor if bandwidth opened up, then agree whether you bring a specific opportunity next time.
brief.confidence: Medium — based on your note about her quieter week and the launch she just shipped.
brief.dontAssume: Do not assume the quiet means depletion — it could be relief or something unresolved she has not named.
